name: ArcadeDB
description: ArcadeDB is a multi-model database that natively supports graphs, documents, key-value, vectors, full-text search, and time-series in a single engine with ACID transactions. It runs embedded inside Java (JVM) and Python applications via the arcadedb-embedded package, which bundles a Java runtime with the wheel for zero-dependency Python use. The same engine can also run as a distributed server with Raft-based HA replication. ArcadeDB supports SQL, OpenCypher (97.8% TCK pass rate), Gremlin, GraphQL, and MongoDB query language, making it the most versatile embedded multi-model database.
format_on_disk: Multi-file (WAL journal + LSM-Tree indexes + columnar time-series chunks)
core_strengths: Five data models in one engine: graph, document, key-value, vector, time-series
Embedded Python via pip with bundled JRE, zero external Java setup required
Native OpenCypher 25 support passing 97.8% of official TCK
JVector-powered vector search with HNSW+DiskANN hybrid and SIMD acceleration
ACID transactions with WAL crash recovery and MVCC concurrency control
Same engine runs embedded, single-node server, or Raft-replicated HA cluster
best_use_cases: GraphRAG/AI pipelines combining vector search with graph traversal in one query
Local-first apps needing document, graph, and vector in one embedded DB
Jupyter notebooks with persistent multi-model storage across kernel restarts
Edge/IoT devices processing sensor time-series alongside graph device topologies
Desktop and CLI tools shipping a full database with zero external dependencies
remarks: ArcadeDB is a conceptual fork of OrientDB by the same founder (Luca Garulli) after SAP's acquisition of OrientDB. The arcadedb-embedded Python package bundles a Java 25 JRE (~73 MB wheel). The Python binding uses JPype to bridge into the JVM engine. The arcadedb-python package is the remote client driver for connecting to an ArcadeDB server; arcadedb-embedded is the in-process package. Wheels are available for Python 3.10-3.14 on Linux (x86_64, ARM64), macOS (Apple Silicon), and Windows (x86_64).
